---
title: 'Energy Forms, Motion, Heat, Light, Sound, Electricity...'
date: '2013-08-24T03:26:00.0020000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Towdah: tÅÂ·dÃ¤'''
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '1353016727122092051'
blogger_filename: '/2013/08/energy-forms-motion-heat-light-sound.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Towdah_ tÅÂ·dÃ¤_/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Misc Research'
---

<div class="tr_bq">
Light, Heat, Sound, and Electricity are all just energy being transferred from one type to another. If God is Light, He is Sound, He IS energy, He is Electricity, He is Heat... </div>
<br />
God SAID: Let there be light... His sound energy released from himself (the very source of all energy) converted into light energy and it's still expanding today. Converting Energy into Mass is scientifically sound, and that's just what God did.<br />
<br />
Sound, Light, Mass... Energy...<br />
<br />
Something to ponder, sounds like good material for a book?<br />
<br />
<ul>
<li><h1 class="margin-bottom-20">
     Forms of Energy: Motion, Heat, Light, Sound    </h1>
</li>
</ul>
<br />
<blockquote>
<a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/05/lit-match.jpg"><img alt="" class=" wp-image-285 alignleft" src="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/05/lit-match-300x202.jpg" style="margin-right: 20px;" width="164" /></a>Energy comes in two basic forms: potential and kinetic.<br />
<br />
<b>Potential Energy</b> is any type of stored energy; it isnâ€™t shown through movement. Potential energy can be <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#chemical_unique">chemical</a>, <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#nuclear_unique">nuclear</a>, <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#gravitational_unique">gravitational</a>, or <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#mechanical_unique">mechanical</a>.<br />
<br />
<a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="kinenergy"></a><b>Kinetic Energy</b> is the energy of movements: the <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#motion_unique">motion</a> of objects (from people to planets), the vibrations of <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/?p=197">atoms</a> by <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#sound_unique">sound waves</a> or in <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#thermal_unique">thermal</a> energy (heat), the <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/forms-of-energy-motion-heat-light-sound-2/#electromagnetic_unique">electromagnetic</a> energy of the movements of light waves, and the motion of electrons in <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/?p=139">electricity</a>.<br />
<br />
Each form of energy can be transformed into any of the other forms, 
but energy isnâ€™t destroyed or created. Losses of energy can always be 
accounted for by small transformations to other types of energy, like 
sound and heat. Power plants convert potential energy or kinetic energy 
into <a href="http://burnanenergyjournal.com/?p=139">electricity</a>, a type of kinetic energy, and electricity<b> </b>in turn can be converted back into other forms of energy, like heat in an oven or light from a lamp.</blockquote>
<br />
<ul>
<li><a href="http://www.absorblearning.com/physics/demo/units/DJFPh082.html" target="_blank">http://www.absorblearning.com/physics/demo/units/DJFPh082.html </a></li>
</ul>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<span class="topictitle">Introduction</span><br />
<br />
Sound and light both
 travel as waves. The properties of these waves differ quite 
considerably. Sound waves travel a million
            times slower than light waves. They have wavelengths between
 1 centimetre and 10 metres, and will easily diffract round corners.
            Light waves have much smaller wavelengths, and only diffract
 through very small holes. This difference is the reason why you
            can often hear things that you cannot see.
         </blockquote>
<br />
<br />
<ul>
<li><span style="color: black;"><a href="http://phys.org/news175766229.html">http://phys.org/news175766229.html</a></span></li>
</ul>
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
(PhysOrg.com) -- 
Researchers at the California Institute of Technology have created a 
nanoscale crystal device that, for the first time, allows scientists to 
confine both light and sound vibrations in the same tiny space.
<br />
<br />
Read more at: <a href="http://phys.org/news175766229.html#jCp">http://phys.org/news175766229.html#jCp</a><br />
&nbsp;
<br />
Optomechanical crystals
 focus on the most basic unitsâ€”or quantaâ€”of light and sound. (These are 
called photons and phonons, respectively.) As Painter notes, there has 
been a rich history of research into both photonic and phononic 
crystals, which use tiny energy traps called bandgaps to capture quanta 
of light or sound within their structures. <br />
<br />
Read more at: <span style="color: black;"><a href="http://phys.org/news175766229.html#jCp">http://phys.org/news175766229.html#jCp</a></span>
</blockquote>
<br />
<ul>
<li><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mass%E2%80%93energy_equivalence">http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mass%E2%80%93energy_equivalence</a></li>
</ul>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
A <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physical_system" title="Physical system">physical system</a>
 has a property called energy and a corresponding property called mass; 
the two properties are equivalent in that they are always both present 
in the same (i.e. constant) proportion to one another. Massâ€“energy 
equivalence arose originally from <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special_relativity" title="Special relativity">special relativity</a>, as developed by <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein" title="Albert Einstein">Albert Einstein</a>, who proposed this equivalence in 1905 in one of his <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Annus_Mirabilis_papers" title="Annus Mirabilis papers"><i>Annus Mirabilis</i> papers</a> entitled "Does the <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inertia" title="Inertia">inertia</a> of an object depend upon its energy-content?"<sup class="reference" id="cite_ref-inertia_1-0"><a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mass%E2%80%93energy_equivalence#cite_note-inertia-1">[1]</a></sup> The equivalence is described by the famous equation:<br />
<dl><dd><img alt="E = mc^2 \,\!" class="tex" src="/images/imported/upload-wikimedia-org/52c7687643df1c12231b39e324850586-02fea74d2e6e.png" /></dd></dl>
where <i>E</i> is energy, <i>m</i> is mass, and <i>c</i> is the <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light" title="Speed of light">speed of light</a>.
 Thus, this mass-energy relation states that the universal 
proportionality factor between equivalent amounts of energy and mass is 
equal to the speed of light squared. This also serves to <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conversion_of_units" title="Conversion of units">convert</a> <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category:Units_of_mass" title="Category:Units of mass">units of mass</a> to <a href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category:Units_of_energy" title="Category:Units of energy">units of energy</a>, no matter what <a class="mw-redirect" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Systems_of_measurement" title="Systems of measurement">system of measurement units</a> is used.</blockquote>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
